perturbation.original_text: FIRST LEVEL OF MAINTENANCE means MORPHO will require the Kuwait Government to contact MORPHO maintenance personnel for all problems associated with the installed Force Field PSS System. MORPHO will respond with fixes and/or workarounds to keep the system operational. MORPHO personnel may document any software problems and refer them to XIMAGE for additional fixes or patches.
perturbation.changed_text: FIRST LEVEL OF MAINTENANCE means MORPHO will directly contact XIMAGE maintenance personnel for certain critical problems associated with the installed Force Field PSS System. MORPHO will respond with fixes and/or workarounds for minor operational issues. MORPHO personnel may document any software problems and refer them to XIMAGE for additional fixes or patches.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding who the Kuwait Government should contact first for maintenance issues. Initially, it was MORPHO maintenance. Now, it specifies contacting XIMAGE directly for 'certain critical problems,' creating ambiguity and contradicting the general instruction for all problems to be handled by MORPHO first. This contradicts section 8 which expects MORPHO to be the first point of contact and a 30 minute response time to Morpho, not Kuwait government.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIMAGE will maintain a 24 hour a day reporting facility to accept calls from MORPHO. XIMAGE agrees to respond to any telephone call made, within 30 minutes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and a contradiction. Originally, all maintenance issues were directed to MORPHO. Now, 'certain critical problems' are to be directed to XIMAGE, contradicting the original instruction. The contradicted text (XIMAGE responding to MORPHO calls) aligns with the original text but clashes with the changed text's direct contact to XIMAGE from the Kuwait Government, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency, falling under 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'. A reasonable reader would immediately recognize this as incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: PROGRAM means ForceField PSS software developed by XIMAGE including the Sybase and Focus software and includes all software provided under the Maintenance Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: PROGRAM means ForceField PSS software developed by XIMAGE and certain third-party software integrated by XIMAGE. For clarity, the Sybase and Focus software included are supported by their respective vendors and not XIMAGE under this agreement, although they are necessary for the ForceField PSS software to function.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction regarding whether Sybase and Focus software are part of the software XIMAGE supports under the maintenance agreement. Initially, they were included. Now, it is stated that they are supported by respective vendors, adding potential dispute about responsibility for their maintenance. This contradicts the meaning of 'Software Maintenance Services' in section 3 where XIMAGE agrees to fix errors in software, where now it is not clear if this includes Sybase and Focus.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIMAGE will provide to MORPHO during the term hereof "Software Maintenance Services" which shall include remedial maintenance service (i.e, error fixing and/or work arounds) for any significant error, malfunction or defect (collectively in "Error") in the Software so that the Software will operate in accordance with the specifications set forth in the related documentation. Correction of Errors is subject to MORPHO's prompt notification to XIMAGE of the nature and description of the Error provided that the Error is not caused by the abuse, misuse or neglect of the products by MORPHO. In addition, XIMAGE will provide the following as additional Customer Support Services: (a) telephone support as reasonably requested by MORPHO at the rate of $100 per hour for all hours in excess of 40 hours in any one-year term; (b) on-site visits to MORPHO's sites as determined to be necessary by Morpho for Error correction, unless error correction is normally performed via Dial up from the XImage facility in San Jose; (c) give title to all modifications and improvements to the PSS Software which XIMAGE generally makes available to its other customers (at no additional Charge) under standard software maintenance agreements relating to the Software. If MORPHO requests XIMAGE to perform any other services, the related terms and conditions shall be based on further separate agreement between the parties. This Agreement and the rights and duties contained herein are not be deemed to cover maintenance services with respect to hardware. Such may be decided in accordance with paragraph 9.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text defines 'PROGRAM' to include Sybase and Focus software, implying that XIMAGE provides maintenance for them. The changed text states that Sybase and Focus are supported by their respective vendors, not XIMAGE, creating ambiguity about XIMAGE's responsibility for their maintenance. This contradicts the 'Software Maintenance Services' defined in the Contradicted Text, specifically the fixing of errors in the 'Software', because it is no longer clear if XIMAGE is responsible for errors in Sybase and Focus. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', where key terms are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: XIMAGE agrees to respond to any telephone call made, within 30 minutes.
perturbation.changed_text: XIMAGE agrees to respond to any telephone call made, within 30 minutes, during XIMAGE's regular business hours.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to Section 8 introduces a contradiction by specifying that the 30-minute response time applies only during XIMAGE's regular business hours. The original statement did not have this limitation, implying a 24-hour response commitment. This change creates uncertainty and potential disputes about response times outside business hours. This introduces a In-Text Contradiction when compared to Section 3 where is being noted 'telephone support as reasonably requested by MORPHO' without any time constraint.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIMAGE will provide to MORPHO during the term hereof "Software Maintenance Services" which shall include remedial maintenance service (i.e, error fixing and/or work arounds) for any significant error, malfunction or defect (collectively in "Error") in the Software so that the Software will operate in accordance with the specifications set forth in the related documentation. Correction of Errors is subject to MORPHO's prompt notification to XIMAGE of the nature and description of the Error provided that the Error is not caused by the abuse, misuse or neglect of the products by MORPHO. In addition, XIMAGE will provide the following as additional Customer Support Services: (a) telephone support as reasonably requested by MORPHO at the rate of $100 per hour for all hours in excess of 40 hours in any one-year term; (b) on-site visits to MORPHO's sites as determined to be necessary by Morpho for Error correction, unless error correction is normally performed via Dial up from the XImage facility in San Jose; (c) give title to all modifications and improvements to the PSS Software which XIMAGE generally makes available to its other customers (at no additional Charge) under standard software maintenance agreements relating to the Software. If MORPHO requests XIMAGE to perform any other services, the related terms and conditions shall be based on further separate agreement between the parties. This Agreement and the rights and duties contained herein are not be deemed to cover maintenance services with respect to hardware. Such may be decided in accordance with paragraph 9.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a contradiction by limiting the 30-minute response time to regular business hours, whereas the original text implied a 24-hour response commitment. This conflicts with the 'telephone support as reasonably requested by MORPHO' clause in the Contradicted Text, which does not specify any time constraints. The added time constraint makes the obligation in the contradicted text unclear and potentially unenforceable, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
